listings.notice_text: Foreclosure Sale TRUSTEE’S SALE OF 4639 HAWORTH ROAD, RICHMOND, VA 23234 In execution of a certain Deed of Trust dated September 10, 2019, in the original principal amount of $60,000.00 recorded in the Clerk’s Office, Circuit Court for Richmond City, Virginia as Instrument No. 190019539. The undersigned Substitute Trustee will offer for sale at public auction in the front of the Circuit Court building for the City of Richmond, John Marshall Courts Building, 400 North 9th Street, Richmond, Virginia, on July 6, 2026, at 2:30 PM, the property described in said Deed of Trust, located at the above address, and more particularly described as follows: ALL THAT CERTAIN LOT, PIECE OR PARCEL OF LAND, WITH IMPROVEMENTS THEREON AND APPURTENANCES THEREUNTO BELONGING, LYING AND BEING IN THE CITY OF RICHMOND, VIRGINIA, (FORMERLY IN THE COUNTY OF CHESTERFIELD, VIRGINIA) KNOWN AND DESIGNATED AS 4639 HAWORTH ROAD, AS MORE PARTICULARLY SHOWN ON THE FOLLOWING PLAT, A COPY OF WHICH IS ATTACHED HERETO AND MADE A PART HEREOF, REFERENCE TO WHICH PLAT IS HEREBY MADE FOR A MORE PARTICULAR DESCRIPTION OF THE REAL ESTATE BEING CONVEYED HEREIN. TERMS OF SALE: ALL CASH. A bidder’s deposit of ten percent (10%) of the sale price or ten percent (10%) of the original principal balance of the subject Deed of Trust, whichever is lower, in the form of cash or certified funds payable to the Substitute Trustee must be present at the time of the sale. The balance of the purchase price will be due within fifteen (15) days of sale, otherwise Purchaser’s deposit may be forfeited to Trustee. Time is of the essence. If the sale is set aside for any reason, the Purchaser at the sale shall be entitled to a return of the deposit paid. The Purchaser may, if provided by the terms of the Trustee’s Memorandum of Foreclosure Sale, be entitled to a $50 cancellation fee from the Substitute Trustee, but shall have no further recourse against the Mortgagor, the Mortgagee or the Mortgagee’s attorney. A form copy of the Trustee’s memorandum of foreclosure sale and contract to purchase real property is available for viewing at www.aldridgepite.com. Additional terms, if any, to be announced at the sale and the Purchaser may be given the option to execute the contract of sale electronically. This is a communication from a debt collector and any information obtained will be used for that purpose. The sale is subject to seller confirmation. Substitute Trustee: Equity Trustees, LLC, 8100 Three Chopt Road, Suite 240, Richmond, VA 23229. If you have any questions or concerns, please contact the Virginia Pre-Sale Department of counsel for Equity Trustees, LLC at 301-961-6555, website: www.aldridgepite.com. VA-380338-1. listings.notice_text: Foreclosure Sale Trustee's Sale 3231 WINNIE DR, SOUTH CHESTERFIELD, VA 23834 (Parcel ID: 796617721400000) Default having been made in the terms of a certain Deed of Trust dated March 1, 2024, in the original principal amount of $280,000.00 and recorded in the Clerk's Office of the Circuit Court of the County of Chesterfield, Virginia on March 4, 2024, in Deed Book 14494, at page 0378, as Instrument Number 240005932, the undersigned Substitute Trustees will sell at public auction on July 09, 2026 at 12:30 PM, in front of the building housing the County of Chesterfield Circuit Court, 9500 Courthouse Road, Chesterfield, VA 23832, the property designated as ALL that certain lot, piece or parcel of land with improvements thereon and appurtenances thereunto belonging, lying and being in Chesterfield County, Virginia, being more particularly known, numbered and designated as Lot 16, Piedmont Estate, as shown on a plat of survey prepared by Townes Site Engineering, dated August 13, 2007, revised December 6, 2007, entitled "Piedmont Estates, Matoaca District, Chesterfield County, Virginia", which plat is recorded in the Clerk`s Office of the Circuit Court of Chesterfield County, Virginia, in Plat Book 189, pages 34-36, reference to which plat is hereby made for a more particular description of the property herein conveyed. TOGETHER WITH an easement of right of way over and upon the streets contained in the Piedmont Estate Subdivision, which streets are labeled as Derrick Lane, Jayme Drive and Winnie Drive and which streets are shown on the plat of Piedmont Estate recorded in Plat Book 189, pages 34-36. Said easement shall be for the purpose of ingress to and egress from Piedmont Avenue and shall remain in effect until the above named streets are accepted into the VDOT road system and made public. BEING the same real estate conveyed to Phillip E. Hopkins by deed to be recorded simultaneous herewith. Sale is subject to all prior liens, easements, restrictions, covenants, and conditions, if any, of record, or other matters which would be disclosed by an accurate survey or inspection of the premises. TERMS: CASH. A deposit of $28,000.00 or 10% of the sale price, whichever is lower, will be required of the successful bidder at time of sale. Prior to the sale, interested bidders will be required to register with and must present a bid deposit which may be held during the sale by the trustee. The bid deposit must be certified funds and/or cash, but no more than $9,900.00 of cash will be accepted. The successful bidder's deposit will be retained at the sale and applied to the sale price. If held by the trustee, all other bid deposits will be returned to the unsuccessful bidders. Settlement is to be made within 15 days. The successful bidder will be responsible for obtaining possession of the property, and for all costs and fees related to recording the Trustee's Deed, including the grantors tax. The successful bidder will be required to execute a Memorandum of Trustee's Sale, available for review upon request before the sale, outlining additional terms of sale and settlement. A Trustee's Deed will be prepared by Trustee's attorney at high bidder's expense. This is a communication from a debt collector. Tromberg, Miller, Morris & Partners, PLLC on behalf of Southeast Equity Trustees, LLC and/or NFPDS-VA LLC, Substitute Trustee(s), 5310 Markel Road, Suite 201, Richmond, VA 23230, File No. 26-000990-VA, Tel: 804-525-1570. The risk of loss or damage to the property passes to the purchaser immediately upon the conclusion of the Substitute Trustee’s sale. This transaction may be subject to the FinCEN Residential Real Estate Reporting Rule (“RRE Rule”) issued pursuant to the Bank Secrecy Act, effective March 1, 2026. Purchaser will be responsible for obtaining the information necessary to comply with the RRE Rule and for providing the necessary information to the Reporting Person, as defined in the RRE Rule within (10) ten days following Ratification of the Sale or Purchaser’s deposit will be forfeited and property resold. In any such event, the sale shall be null and void, and the Purchaser’s sole remedy, in law or equity, shall be the return of the Purchaser’s deposit without interest. Local time In execution of that certain Purchase Money Deed of Trust and Assignment of Leases and Rents dated January 26, 2024, and recorded on January 29, 2024, in the Clerk’s Office of the Circuit Court for the City of Richmond, Virginia, at Instrument No. 240001290, (the “Deed of Trust”), default having been made in the payment of the debt secured by the Deed of Trust, and being required to do so by the noteholder, the undersigned Substitute Trustees (either of whom may act) will offer for sale at public auction just outside the entrance to the Circuit Court for the City of Richmond, Virginia, 400 N. 9 th Street, Richmond, Virginia 232196 on June 10, 2026, at 1:00 p.m. local time, the real property, improvements, and personal property described below. All those certain tracts, pieces, or parcels of land located at 1501 ½ West Cary Street, Richmond, VA 23220 (also known as 1501 West Cary Street) ( Parcel ID: W0000603015) and 1503 West Cary Street, Richmond, VA 23220 (Parcel ID: W0000603014), plus all rights, title and interest in the easements, water line and sewer line serving the aforesaid property and more particularly described in the aforesaid Deed of Trust to which reference is hereby made (the “Land”); all structures, buildings and improvements of every kind and description, now located on the Land (the “Improvements”); and all personal property owned by JSGBSM LLC, a Virginia limited liability company, and which is located on the Land or which JSGBSM, LLC stores within the Improvements, including any and all personal property, and which noteholder has a security interest pursuant to a Pledge and Security Agreement dated January 26, 2024 (the “Personalty”). The Land, Improvements, and Personalty shall be collectively referred to herein after as the “Property.” The acting Substitute Trustee’s sale shall be made subject to all existing superior liens, easements and restrictive covenants, if any, as the same may lawfully affect the Property. Terms of Sale : All Cash. The Property will be sold “AS IS, WHERE IS” and “WITH ALL FAULTS” without representations or warranties with respect to the Property including, without limitation, representations or warranties as to the structural integrity, physical condition, construction, workmanship, materials, habitability, fitness for particular purpose, merchantability, or non-infringement of all or part of the Property. A bidder’s deposit will be required at the date of the sale in cash or by certified or cashier’s check payable to the Substitute Trustee from the successful bidder in the amount no less than 10% of the bid. The successful bidder will have two (2) business days to deliver to the Substitute Trustee certified funds equal to ten percent (10%) of the successful bid. The balance of the purchase price shall be paid by certified check or wire transfer of immediately available funds (provided that any successful credit bid by the beneficiary of the Deed of Trust shall be applied against the purchase price), and settlement shall be held on or before twenty-one (21) days from the date of the sale at the office of the Substitute Trustee, time being of the essence. The successful bidder’s deposit, without interest thereon, shall be applied to the credit of the successful bidder at settlement. If the successful bidder fails to complete settlement, the deposit shall be forfeited and applied to the costs of the sale including Substitute Trustees’ fees, with the remaining balance to be applied against the debt secured by the Deed of Trust, and the Property shall be resold at the risk and cost of the defaulting successful bidder. Such forfeiture shall not limit any rights or remedies of the Substitute Trustees or the beneficiary under the Deed of Trust with respect to any such default by the successful bidder. Time shall be of the essence with respect to the settlement date. The settlement shall be held at the offices of the acting Substitute Trustee or at such other place as may be agreed to by the acting Substitute Trustee. The acting Substitute Trustee will execute a special warranty deed for the Land and Improvements, and a bill of sale, without warranties, for the Personalty, but the acting Substitute Trustee will not be required to deliver possession of the Property. All loss or damage to the Property by fire, windstorm or other casualty or by condemnation from and after the time of sale and before settlement shall be at the risk of the successful bidder. Real estate taxes, water rents, stormwater fees and all other public charges and assessments shall be adjusted for the current period to the date of sale (not settlement), and the purchaser shall be responsible for payment of such taxe

listings.notice_text: Foreclosure Sale TRUSTEE SALE OF 3316 Maryland Avenue, Richmond, VA 23222 In execution of the Deed of Trust dated October 30, 2020, in the original principal amount of $173,302.00, recorded as Inst. 200025997, assigned as Inst. 260005149, in the Clerk’s Office of the Circuit Court for the City of Richmond, Virginia, default having occurred in the payment of the indebtedness thereby secured and at the request of the holder of the note, the undersigned, as Substitute Trustee, will sell at public auction at the front entrance of the Circuit Court for the City of Richmond on June 18, 2026, at 11:45 a.m., the property located at the above address and described as Lot 18, Block 21, Highland Park. TERMS: CASH. PROPERTY SOLD AS IS WITH SPECIAL WARRANTY OF TITLE. A deposit of $15,000.00 or 10% of the successful bid amount (whichever is lower) will be required immediately of the successful bidder, in cashier’s check or certified funds only payable to or signed over to “DOLANREID PLLC, TRUSTEE”, no personal checks accepted. Balance is due within 15 days from date of sale VIA BANKWIRE ONLY. Sale is subject to post sale confirmation and audit of the status of the loan including, but not limited to, determination of whether the borrower filed for and obtained bankruptcy protection, entered into any repayment agreement, reinstated or paid off the loan prior to the sale. In any such event, or if trustee is unable to complete the sale, or if it is set aside or not fully completed for any reason except purchaser’s default, the sale shall be null and void, and the purchaser’s sole remedy, in law or equity, shall be the return of the deposit without interest. Upon purchaser’s default, the deposit shall be forfeited and the property resold at the risk and costs of the defaulting purchaser. Sale is subject to all prior liens, easements, restrictions, covenants, reservations and conditions, if any, of record, as well as inchoate liens and any other matters which would be disclosed by an accurate survey or inspection of the premises. Real estate taxes will be adjusted to the date of sale. All costs of conveyance, deed, examination of title, recording charges, grantor’s tax and possession will be at the expense of the purchaser. All risks of casualty immediately pass to the successful bidder. Time is of the essence. Winning bidder must sign a memorandum of sale immediately upon completion of sale. Additional terms may be announced at the time of sale. Trustee’s affidavit and a copy of the notice of sale available at the foreclosure sale. The opening bid is not announced until the sale. Current sale status available at dolanreid.com/foreclosure-sales. THIS IS A COMMUNICATION FROM A DEBT COLLECTOR. ANY INFORMATION OBTAINED WILL BE USED FOR THAT PURPOSE. For Information Contact: DolanReid PLLC, Substitute Trustee(s) 12610 Patrick Henry Dr., Ste. D, Newport News, VA 23602; (757) 320-0255, Ext. 220 or Ext. 260 Publish On: May 29 & June 5, 2026 COL-7006656 2026-06-05

June 25, 2026 at 10:00 AM improved real property, with an abbreviated legal description of the following described property, to wit: ALL that certain lot, piece or parcel of land, with the improvements thereon and the appurtenances thereunto belonging, lying and being in Bermuda District, Chesterfield County, Virginia, and designated as Lot 51, and shown on that certain plat entitled "FOREST LAKE, SECTION C, Bermuda District, Chesterfield County, Virginia", made by Timmons, dated December 17, 2001, and recorded March 27, 2002, in the Clerk`s Office, Circuit Court, Chesterfield County, Virginia, in Plat Book 124, Pages 94 through 99, reference to which plat is hereby made for a more particular description. AND as more fully described in the aforesaid Deed of Trust. TERMS OF SALE: The property will be sold “AS IS,” WITHOUT REPRESENTATION OR WARRANTY OF ANY KIND AND SUBJECT TO conditions, restrictions, reservations, easements, rights of way, and all other matters of record taking priority over the Deed of Trust to be announced at the time of sale. A deposit of $20,000 or 10% of the sale price, whichever is lower, will be required at the time of sale, in the form of certified check, cashier's check or money order by the purchaser. The balance of the purchase price, with interest at the rate contained in the Deed of Trust Note from the date of sale to the date said funds are received in the office of the SUBSTITUTE TRUSTEE, will be due within fifteen (15) days of sale. In the event of default by the successful bidder, the entire deposit shall be forfeited and applied to the costs and expenses of sale and Substitute Trustee's fee. All other public charges or assessments, including water/sewer charges, whether incurred prior to or after the sale, and all other costs incident to settlement to be paid by the purchaser. In the event taxes, any other public charges have been advanced, a credit will be due to the seller, to be adjusted from the date of sale at the time of settlement. Purchaser agrees to pay the seller's attorneys at settlement, a fee of $470.00 for review of the settlement documents. Additional terms will be announced at the time of sale and the successful bidder will be required to execute and deliver to the Substitute Trustees a memorandum or contract of the sale at the conclusion of bidding. FOR INFORMATION CONTACT Rosenberg & Associates, LLC (Attorney for the Secured Party) 4340 East West Highway, Suite 600 Bethesda, MD 20814 301-907-8000 www.rosenberg-assoc.com COL-7006619 2026-06-04

listings.notice_text: Foreclosure Sale Trustee Sale Of 3829 Pinewood Glen Lane, Richmond, VA 23223 Henrico County In execution of a Deed of Trust in the original principal amount of $155,700.00, dated March 2, 2006 recorded in the Clerk's Office of the Circuit Court of the Henrico County, Virginia, in Document No. 15629, in Book No. 4086, at Page 2027, at the request of the holder of the Note, the undersigned Substitute Trustee and will offer for sale at public auction at the entrance to the Circuit Court Building of the County of Henrico, 4301 East Parham Road, Henrico, VA 23273, on July 7, 2026 at 10:30 AM the property described in said deed, located at the above address and briefly described as: Lot 1, Block A, Section 1, Pinewood Glen, with any improvements thereon Subject to any and all covenants, conditions, restrictions, easements, and all other matters of record taking priority over the Deed of Trust, if any, affecting the aforesaid property. TERMS OF SALE: CASH: A deposit of $20,000.00 or 10% of the sales price, whichever is lower, cash or certified check will be required at the time of sale, but no more than $9,000.00 of cash will be accepted, with settlement within fifteen (15) days from the date of sale. Sale is subject to post sale confirmation that the borrower did not file for protection under the U.S. Bankruptcy Code prior to the sale which affects the validity of the sale, as well as to post-sale confirmation of the status of the loan with the loan servicer including, but not limited to, determination of whether the borrower entered into any repayment agreement, reinstated or paid off the loan prior to the sale. Sale is also subject to buyer providing Trustee with information needed for reporting requirements required by Federal Law including FinCen requirements (31 CFR Chapter X) within 15 days from date of sale. In any such event, the sale shall be null and void, and the Purchaser’s sole remedy, in law or equity, shall be the return of his deposit without interest. Additional terms may be announced at the time of sale. listings.notice_text: Foreclosure Sale Trustee's Sale 3900 Decatur Street, Richmond, VA 23224 (Parcel ID: C0060158016 and S0002132014) Default having been made in the terms of a certain Deed of Trust dated August 12, 2022, in the original principal amount of $1,275,400.00 and recorded in the Clerk's Office of the Circuit Court of the City of Richmond, Virginia on August 15, 2022, as Instrument Number 220018709, the undersigned Substitute Trustees will sell at public auction on June 05, 2026 at 3:45 PM, in front of the building housing the City of Richmond Circuit Court, 400 N. 9th Street, Richmond, VA 23219, the property designated as All that certain It piece or parcel of land, with the improvements thereon and appurtenances thereto belonging, lying and being in the City of Richmond (formerly Manchester Magisterial District, Chesterfield County), Virginia, known as 3900 Decatur Street and designated as Lot No. 22 in Block 3, on the plan of Woodstock which said plan or plat thereof is recorded in the Clerk`s Office of the Circuit Court of Chesterfield County, Virginia, in Plat Book 4, Pages 58 and 59. Sale is subject to all prior liens, easements, restrictions, covenants, and conditions, if any, of record, or other matters which would be disclosed by an accurate survey or inspection of the premises. TERMS: CASH. A deposit of $128,000.00 or 10% of the sale price, whichever is lower, will be required of the successful bidder at time of sale. Prior to the sale, interested bidders will be required to register with and must present a bid deposit which may be held during the sale by the trustee. The bid deposit must be certified funds and/or cash, but no more than $9,900.00 of cash will be accepted. The successful bidder's deposit will be retained at the sale and applied to the sale price. If held by the trustee, all other bid deposits will be returned to the unsuccessful bidders. Settlement is to be made within 15 days. The successful bidder will be responsible for obtaining possession of the property, and for all costs and fees related to recording the Trustee's Deed, including the grantors tax. The successful bidder will be required to execute a Memorandum of Trustee's Sale, available for review upon request before the sale, outlining additional terms of sale and settlement. A Trustee's Deed will be prepared by Trustee's attorney at high bidder's expense. This is a communication from a debt collector. Tromberg, Miller, Morris & Partners, PLLC on behalf of Southeast Equity Trustees, LLC and/or NFPDS-VA LLC, Substitute Trustee(s), 5310 Markel Road, Suite 201, Richmond, VA 23230, File No. 25-001336, Tel: 804-525-1570. listings.notice_text: Foreclosure Sale COMMISSIONER’S SALE – NOTICE OF DEFAULT AND FORECLOSURE SALE 807 Erich Road, Richmond, Virginia 23225 NOTICE OF DEFAULT AND FORECLOSURE SALE, made this Sixth day of May, 2026, by Richard A. Lash, Foreclosure Commissioner: WHEREAS, on October 16, 2007, a certain Deed of Trust was executed by Martha Rosser as grantor in favor of Live Well Financial, Inc. as beneficiary and Titleworks of VA as trustee, and was recorded on October 25, 2007, as Instrument No. 070035523 in the Land Records of the City of Richmond, VA; and WHEREAS, the Deed of Trust was insured by the United States Secretary of Housing and Urban Development (the “Secretary” or “HUD”) pursuant to the National Housing Act for the purpose of providing single family housing; and WHEREAS, the beneficial interest in the Deed of Trust is now owned by the Secretary, pursuant to an assignment dated October 25, 2018, and recorded on January 2, 2019 as Instrument No. 190000003 in the office of the Land Records of the City of Richmond, VA; and WHEREAS, a default has been made in the covenants and conditions of the Deed of Trust due to violation of 9(a)(i): A borrower dies and the property is not the principal residence of at least one surviving borrower; and WHEREAS, the entire amount delinquent as of April 23, 2026 is $212,447.36; and WHEREAS, by virtue of this default, the Secretary has declared the entire amount of the indebtedness secured by the Deed of Trust to be immediately due and payable; NOW THEREFORE, pursuant to powers vested in me by the Single Family Mortgage Foreclosure Act of 1994, 12 U.S.C. 3751 et seq., by 24 CFR part 27, subpart B, and by the Secretary’s designation of Buonassissi, Henning & Lash, P.C. as Foreclosure Commissioner, recorded on September 8, 2023 in Instrument No. 230013630, notice is hereby given that on June 8, 2026 at 2:30 PM local time, all real and personal property at or used in connection with the following described premises (“Property”) will be sold at public auction to the highest bidder: Commonly known as: All that certain lot, piece or parcel of land, together with all improvements thereon and appurtenances thereunto belonging, lying and being in Manchester Magisterial District, City of Richmond (formerly Chesterfield County), Virginia, and being known and described as Lot 9, Block B, on the plan of Westover Manor, plat of which was made by Laprade Bros., Civil Engineers & Surveyors, dated September 22, 1958 and recorded October 20, 1958 and recorded October 20, 1958, in Plat Book 10, Pages 113 and 114, Clerk's Office, Circuit Court, Chesterfield County, Virginia. It being the same property conveyed to Martha Rosser, widow by Deed from Byron E. Howlett and Hazel R. Howlett, husband and wife and Michael C. Rosser, unmarried dated July 6, 2007, recorded July 31, 2007, in the Clerk's Office of the Circuit Court of Richmond City, Virginia as Instrument No. 070026022. The said Martha P. Rosser departed this life on or about December 31, 2021. The sale will be held at the front of the Circuit Court for the City of Richmond, John Marshall Courts Building, 400 North Ninth Street, Richmond, Virginia. The Secretary of Housing and Urban Development will bid $219,586.35. There will be no proration of taxes, rents or other income or liabilities, except that the purchaser will pay, at or before closing, his prorata share of any real estate taxes that have been paid by the Secretary to the date of the foreclosure sale. When making their bids, all bidders except the Secretary must submit a deposit 10% of the successful bid in the form of a certified check or cashier’s check made out to the United States Secretary of Housing and Urban Development. A deposit need not accompany each oral bid. If the successful bid is oral, a deposit of 10% of the successful bid must be presented before the bidding is closed. The deposit is nonrefundable. The remainder of the purchase price must be delivered within 30 days of the sale or at such other time as the Secretary may determine for good cause shown, time being of the essence. This amount, like the bid deposits, must be delivered in the form of a certified or cashier’s check. If the Secretary is the highest bidder, he need not pay the bid amount in cash. The successful bidder will pay all conveying fees, all real estate and other taxes that are due on or after the delivery date of the remainder of the payment and all other costs associated with the transfer of title. At the conclusion of the sale, the deposits of the unsuccessful bidders will be returned to them. The Secretary may grant an extension of time within which to deliver the remainder of the payment. All extensions will be for 15-day increments for a fee of $500.00, paid in advance. The extension fee shall be in the form of a certified or cashier’s check made payable to the Secretary of HUD. If the high bidder closes the sale prior to the expiration of any extension period, the unused portion of the extension fee sha

listings.notice_text: Foreclosure Sale Trustee's Sale NOTICE OF SUBSTITUTE TRUSTEE’S SALE ( 6965 Sir Galahad Road, Henrico, VA 23231 ) Tax Map Ref. No.: 824-692-5434 Pursuant to the terms of a Deed of Trust dated March 14, 2024, recorded in the Clerk’s Office of the Henrico County, Virginia Circuit Court (the “Clerk’s Office”) on March 21, 2024 as Instrument No. 202400006536, Book 06547, Page 1353 (the “Deed of Trust”), the real property described in the Deed of Trust commonly known as 6965 Sir Galahad Road, Henrico, VA 23231 and the improvements thereon, (the “Real Property”), along with any personal property described in the Deed of Trust, including, without limitation, any equipment, fixtures and other articles of personal property attached or affixed to the Real Property (the “Personal Property,” and together with the Real Property, the “Property”), will be offered for sale at public auction. A description of the Real Property is set forth below and more specifically described in the Deed of Trust, to which reference is hereby made: All that certain lot, piece, or parcel of land, together with improvements thereon and appurtenances thereunto belonging, lying and being in Henrico County, Virginia, Varina District, known as Lot 73, Block F, Section 5, on that certain plat of subdivision entitled Castleton Section 5 A Resubdivision of Castleton Section 1 Future Development Varina District Henrico County, Virginia made by Townes Site Engineering dated June 10, 2020, and recorded in the Clerk's Office on July 16, 2020 in Plat Book 135, Page 214. The sale of the Property will take place at 11:00 a.m. on June 17, 2026 at the entrance to the Henrico County Virginia Circuit Court, 4309 E. Parham Rd, Richmond, VA 23228 . Terms: The purchase price in the form of immediately available funds will be due and payable on the closing date. A deposit in the form of cash, cashier’s check, or other immediately available funds in the amount of 10% of the bid amount may be required of the successful bidder at the time of sale. Bidders’ deposits must be shown to the Substitute Trustee for verification prior to the start of the public auction. The successful bidder will be required to close within thirty (30) days of the date of sale. Time will be of the essence as to the closing date and the payment of the purchase price. All loss or damage to the Property from the time of sale and before settlement shall be at the risk of the successful bidder. The Property will be sold “AS IS” and will be conveyed by Special Warranty Deed, subject to all rights, reservations, leases, covenants, conditions, easements and restrictions, as they may lawfully affect the Property. The purchaser at the sale will be required to pay all closing costs, including the Grantor’s recording tax. Real estate taxes will be pro-rated as of the foreclosure sale date. Additional terms may be announced at the sale. Daniel S. Oberski, Substitute Trustee For information contact: Daniel S. AND as more fully described in the aforesaid Deed of Trust. TERMS OF SALE: The property will be sold “AS IS,” WITHOUT REPRESENTATION OR WARRANTY OF ANY KIND AND SUBJECT TO conditions, restrictions, reservations, easements, rights of way, and all other matters of record taking priority over the Deed of Trust to be announced at the time of sale. A deposit of $20,000 or 10% of the sale price, whichever is lower, will be required at the time of sale, in the form of certified check, cashier's check or money order by the purchaser. In any such event, or if trustee is unable to complete the sale, or if it is set aside or not fully completed for any reason except purchaser’s default, the sale shall be null and void, and the purchaser’s sole remedy, in law or equity, shall be the return of the deposit without interest. Upon purchaser’s default, the deposit shall be forfeited and the property resold at the risk and costs of the defaulting purchaser. Sale is subject to all prior liens, easements, restrictions, covenants, reservations and conditions, if any, of record, as well as inchoate liens and any other matters which would be disclosed by an accurate survey or inspection of the premises. Real estate taxes will be adjusted to the date of sale. All costs of conveyance, deed, examination of title, recording charges, grantor’s tax and possession will be at the expense of the purchaser. All risks of casualty immediately pass to the successful bidder. Time is of the essence. Winning bidder must sign a memorandum of sale immediately upon completion of sale. Additional terms may be announced at the time of sale. Trustee’s affidavit and a copy of the notice of sale available at the foreclosure sale. The opening bid is not announced until the sale. Current sale status available at dolanreid.com/foreclosure-sales. THIS IS A COMMUNICATION FROM A DEBT COLLECTOR. ANY INFORMATION OBTAINED WILL BE USED FOR THAT PURPOSE. For Information Contact: DolanReid PLLC, Substitute Trustee(s) 12610 Patrick Henry Dr., Ste. D, Newport News, VA 23602; (757) 320-0255, Ext. 220 or Ext. 260 Publish On: May 15 & 22, 2026 COL-7006529 2026-05-22

listings.notice_text: Foreclosure Sale Trustee's Sale 3209 Stockton Street, Richmond, VA 23224 (Parcel ID: S-000-2132-014) Default having been made in the terms of a certain Deed of Trust dated August 12, 2022, in the original principal amount of $1,275,400.00 and recorded in the Clerk's Office of the Circuit Court of the City of Richmond, Virginia on August 15, 2022, as Instrument Number 220018709, the undersigned Substitute Trustees will sell at public auction on May 22, 2026 at 3:45 PM, in front of the building housing the City of Richmond Circuit Court, 400 N. 9th Street, Richmond, VA 23219, the property designated as All that certain lot, piece or parcel of land with the improvements thereon, and appurtenances thereon belonging, lying and being in the City of Richmond (formerly Chesterfield County), Virginia, being known and designated as Lot 11, Block 10, Weisiger`s Addition, recorded in the Clerk`s Office, Circuit Court, Chesterfield County, Virginia, in Plat Book 2, page 312, to which plat reference is hereby made for a more particular description. Sale is subject to all prior liens, easements, restrictions, covenants, and conditions, if any, of record, or other matters which would be disclosed by an accurate survey or inspection of the premises. TERMS: CASH. A deposit of $128,000.00 or 10% of the sale price, whichever is lower, will be required of the successful bidder at time of sale. Prior to the sale, interested bidders will be required to register with and must present a bid deposit which may be held during the sale by the trustee. The bid deposit must be certified funds and/or cash, but no more than $9,900.00 of cash will be accepted. The successful bidder's deposit will be retained at the sale and applied to the sale price. If held by the trustee, all other bid deposits will be returned to the unsuccessful bidders. Settlement is to be made within 15 days. The successful bidder will be responsible for obtaining possession of the property, and for all costs and fees related to recording the Trustee's Deed, including the grantors tax. The successful bidder will be required to execute a Memorandum of Trustee's Sale, available for review upon request before the sale, outlining additional terms of sale and settlement. A Trustee's Deed will be prepared by Trustee's attorney at high bidder's expense. This is a communication from a debt collector. Tromberg, Miller, Morris & Partners, PLLC on behalf of Southeast Equity Trustees, LLC, Substitute Trustee(s), 5310 Markel Road, Suite 201, Richmond, VA 23230, File No. 25-001337, Tel: 804-525-1570. COL-7006641 2026-05-20

A description of the Real Property is set forth below and more specifically described in the Deed of Trust, to which reference is hereby made: ALL that certain lot, piece, or parcel of land, together with improvements thereon and appurtenances thereunto belonging, lying and being in Henrico County, Virginia, Varina District, known as Lot 29, Block D, Section 6, on that certain plat of subdivision entitled CASTLETON SECTION 6 A RESUBDIVISION OF CASTLETON SECTION 1 FUTURE DEVELOPMENT VARINA DISTRICT HENRICO COUNTY, VIRGINIA made by Townes Site Engineering dated November 2, 2021, and recorded in the Clerk’s Office, Circuit Court, Henrico County, Virginia on October 14, 2022, in Plat Book 137, Pages 311-318. The sale will take place at 11:00 a.m. on May 27, 2026 at the entrance to the Henrico Virginia Circuit Court, 4309 E. Parham Rd, Richmond, VA 23228. Terms: The purchase price in the form of immediately available funds will be due and payable on the closing date. A deposit in the form of immediately available funds in the amount of 10% of the bid amount may be required of the successful bidder at the time of sale. Bidders’ deposits must be shown to the Substitute Trustee for verification prior to the start of the public auction. The successful bidder will be required to close within thirty (30) days of the date of sale. Time will be of the essence as to the closing date and the payment of the purchase price. All loss or damage to the Property from the time of sale and before settlement shall be at the risk of the successful bidder. The Property will be sold “AS IS” and will be conveyed by Special Warranty Deed, subject to all rights, reservations, leases, covenants, conditions, easements, and restrictions, as they may lawfully affect the Property. Neither the Substitute Trustee nor the holder of the note secured by the Deed of Trust will deliver physical possession of the Property to the successful bidder. The purchaser at the sale will be required to pay all closing costs, including the Grantor’s recording tax. Real estate taxes will be pro-rated as of the foreclosure sale date. Additional terms may be announced at the sale. Daniel S. Oberski Substitute Trustee For information contact: Daniel S. listings.notice_text: Foreclosure Sale Trustee's Sale of 250 St. Mark Street and 2847 Shady Hill Rd, Petersburg, VA TRUSTEE'S SALE OF PROPERTY 250 Saint Mark Street Petersburg, VA 23803 (Petersburg City) Parcel ID: 044110002 2847 Shady Hill Road Petersburg, VA 23803 (Petersburg City) Parcel ID: 049040022 In execution of a Deed of Trust dated December 18, 2024, recorded in the Clerk's Office of the Circuit Court of the City of Petersburg, Virginia, as Instrument Number 202404428, Justin Smart, Esq. for Day Law Group, PLLC, Trustee, will offer for sale at public auction in front of the building housing the Petersburg Circuit Court at 7 Courthouse Ave, Peterburg, VA 23803, the 29th day of May, 2026, at 1:00 p.m., the two parcels separately, being the properties described in said Deed of Trust, lying in the City of Petersburg, Virginia, and more particularly described as follows: Parcel 1: All that certain lot of parcel of land with improvements thereon lying, being and situate on the south side of St. Mark Street in the City of Petersburg, State of Virginia, and further described as follows: Beginning at a point on the southern line of St. Marl Street a distance of One Hundred Nineteen (119) feet from the southeastern intersection of High Pearl Street and St. Mark Street, running thence along the southern line of St. Mark Street in a direction N. 84 degrees East and fronting on the said street a distance of about Twenty-five (25) feet; thence extending back between lines almost parallel in a southerly direction on its eastern line One Hundred Thirty-one and three-tenths (131.3) feet, more or less, and on its western line One Hundred Twenty-Seven (127) feet, more or less. The aforesaid property is designated by current street No. 250 St. Mark Street, and is the eastern portion of Lot No. Four (4) on a map showing property known as "Map Showing Property Situate on High Pearl and St. Mark Streets", dated April 21-22, 1943, made by John W. Pugh, Ref. Engr., and recorded in the Clerk's Office of the Hustings Court of the City of Petersburg in Deed Book 145, Page 119, along with a certain deed dated April 23, 1943, from Thomas M. Young and wife to R.A. Condrey. It being the same property conveyed unto Secic Real-Estate Investments LLC on deed dated December 18, 2024, and recorded in the Clerk's Office for the Circuit Court of the City of Petersburg, Virginia as Instrument Number 202404427. This conveyance is made subject to all valid and existing conditions, easements, reservations and restrictions heretofore imposed upon said property by recorded deed or plat and now binding thereon. Parcel 2: ALL that certain lot or parcel of land, being and situate in the City of Petersburg, Virginia, Formerly Dinwiddie County, Virginia, designated as the northwest portion of Lot 4, Block D, Dunedin as shown on a plat entitled "Plat Showing Property to be Acquired by Helen V. Mayes on the East Side of Shady Hill Lane", made by Richard A. Bristow, dated October 13, 1972, and recorded in the Clerk's Office, Circuit Court, City of Petersburg, Virginia, in Deed Book 317 page 315, and more particularly described as follows: BEGINNING at a point 25.2 feet east of the center line of Shady Hill Lane, and thence in a northerly direction N. 17 degrees 20 minutes west 201.12 feet to a point; thence in an easterly direction N. 79 degrees 05 minuted 200 feet east to a point; thence in a southerly direction south 17 degrees 20 minutes east 201.12 feet to appoint; thence in a westerly direction south 79 degrees 05 minutes west 200 feet to the point of beginning; the property intended to be conveyed by this deed fronts 201.2 feet on the east side of Shady Hill Lane and extends back between parallel lines 200 feet and is bounded on the south by property conveyed Thomas C. Ammerman by Clinton E. Fable and Kathryn C. Fable, et als, by deed dated June 24, 1955, duly od record in the Clerk's Office of the Clerk Court of Dinwiddie County, Virginia, in Deed Book 92 at Page 3527 and on the north by Lot 3, Block D, on a plat of Dunedin, recorded in the Clerk's Office of the Circuit Court of Dinwiddie County, Virginia, in Plat Book 1, at Page 474. It being the same parcel that is referenced in a deed conveyed unto Secic Real-Estate Investments LLC dated March 5, 2024, and recorded in the Clerk's Office for the Circuit Court of the City of Petersburg, Virginia as Instrument Number 24000422. This conveyance is made subject to all valid and existing conditions, easements, reservations and restrictions heretofore imposed upon said property by recorded deed or plat and now binding thereon. TERMS: Cash - Bidder's deposit of ten per cent (10%) of the sales price by cashier's or certified check required. Balance of purchase price shall be due and payable no later than fifteen (15) days after sale. THESE TWO PARCELS WILL BE SOLD SEPERATELY. Day Law Group PLLC For Information Contact: Justin C. Smart, Esq. 1019 Vista Park Drive, Suite A Forest, Va. 24551 434-528-8877 NOTE TO PUBLISHER:

listings.notice_text: Foreclosure Sale TRUSTEE’S SALE OF 5550 BAYBRANCH XING, DISPUTANTA, VA 23842 In execution of a certain Deed of Trust dated March 26, 2020, in the original principal amount of $288,000.00 recorded in the Clerk’s Office, Circuit Court for Prince George County, Virginia as Instrument No. 200000955. The undersigned Substitute Trustee will offer for sale at public auction in the front of the Circuit Court building for Petersburg City, 8 Courthouse Avenue, Petersburg, Virginia, or any such temporary alternative Circuit Court location designated by the Judges of the Circuit Court, on July 13, 2026, at 1:15 PM, the property described in said Deed of Trust, located at the above address, and more particularly described as follows: ALL THAT CERTAIN LOT OR PARCEL OF LAND, WITH THE IMPROVEMENTS THEREON AND APPURTENANCES THERETO BELONGING, LYING AND BEING IN BRANDON MAGISTERIAL DISTRICT, PRINCE GEORGE COUNTY, VIRGINIA, DESIGNATED AS LOT 14, ON THE PLAT OF "GREENBRIAR, SECTION TWO, BRANDON DISTRICT, PRINCE GEORGE COUNTY, VIRGINIA", IN PLAT BOOK 24, PAGES 85-89, TO WHICH PLAT REFERENCE IS HEREBY MADE FOR A MORE PARTICULAR DESCRIPTION. TERMS OF SALE: ALL CASH. A bidder’s deposit of ten percent (10%) of the sale price or ten percent (10%) of the original principal balance of the subject Deed of Trust, whichever is lower, in the form of cash or certified funds payable to the Substitute Trustee must be present at the time of the sale. The balance of the purchase price will be due within fifteen (15) days of sale, otherwise Purchaser’s deposit may be forfeited to Trustee. Time is of the essence. If the sale is set aside for any reason, the Purchaser at the sale shall be entitled to a return of the deposit paid. The Purchaser may, if provided by the terms of the Trustee’s Memorandum of Foreclosure Sale, be entitled to a $50 cancellation fee from the Substitute Trustee, but shall have no further recourse against the Mortgagor, the Mortgagee or the Mortgagee’s attorney. A form copy of the Trustee’s memorandum of foreclosure sale and contract to purchase real property is available for viewing at www.aldridgepite.com. Additional terms, if any, to be announced at the sale and the Purchaser may be given the option to execute the contract of sale electronically. This is a communication from a debt collector and any information obtained will be used for that purpose. The sale is subject to seller confirmation. Substitute Trustee: Equity Trustees, LLC, 8100 Three Chopt Road, Suite 240, Richmond, VA 23229. If you have any questions or concerns, please contact the Virginia Pre-Sale Department of counsel for Equity Trustees, LLC at 301-961-6555, website: www.aldridgepite.com. VA-378139-1. listings.notice_text: Foreclosure Sale Trustee Sale: 95659, 4707 Five Springs Ct TRUSTEE SALE 4707 Five Springs Ct, Midlothian, VA 23112 Chesterfield County In execution of a Deed of Trust in the original principal amount of $72,300.00, dated October 8, 2004 recorded in the Clerk's Office of the Circuit Court of the Chesterfield County, Virginia, in Document No. 77026, in Book No. 6066, at Page 365, at the request of the holder of the Note, the undersigned Substitute Trustee and will offer for sale at public auction at the entrance to the Circuit Court of Chesterfield County, 9500 Courthouse Road, Chesterfield, on June 9, 2026 at 10:30 AM the property described in said deed, located at the above address and briefly described as: Lot 13, Five Springs, Phase 1, with any improvements thereon Subject to any and all covenants, conditions, restrictions, easements, and all other matters of record taking priority over the Deed of Trust, if any, affecting the aforesaid property. TERMS OF SALE: CASH: A deposit of $20,000.00 or 10% of the sales price, whichever is lower, cash or certified check will be required at the time of sale, but no more than $9,000.00 of cash will be accepted, with settlement within fifteen (15) days from the date of sale. Sale is subject to post sale confirmation that the borrower did not file for protection under the U.S. Bankruptcy Code prior to the sale which affects the validity of the sale, as well as to post-sale confirmation of the status of the loan with the loan servicer including, but not limited to, determination of whether the borrower entered into any repayment agreement, reinstated or paid off the loan prior to the sale. Sale is also subject to buyer providing Trustee with information needed for reporting requirements required by Federal Law including FinCen requirements (31 CFR Chapter X) within 15 days from date of sale. In any such event, the sale shall be null and void, and the Purchaser’s sole remedy, in law or equity, shall be the return of his deposit without interest. Additional terms may be announced at the time of sale. Pursuant to the Federal Fair Debt Collection Practices Act, we advise you that this firm is a debt collector attempting to collect the indebtedness referred to herein and any information we obtain will be used for that purpose. SAMUEL I. WHITE, P.C., Substitute Trustee This is a communication from a debt collector. FOR INFORMATION CONTACT: SAMUEL I. WHITE, P.C. (95659) 448 Viking Drive Suite 350 Virginia Beach, VA 23452 757-457-1460 - Call Between 9:00 a.m. and 5:00 p.m. or visit our website at www.siwpc.net To: Richmond Times COL-7006568 2026-05-16

listings.notice_text: Foreclosure Sale Trustee's Sale 4011 Barth Road, Richmond, Virginia 23234 (PIN: C0081144012) Default having been made in the terms of a certain Deed of Trust dated December 24, 2004, in the original principal amount of $49,500.00 and recorded in the Clerk's Office of the Circuit Court of the City of Richmond, Virginia in Instrument No. 050002655, the undersigned Substitute Trustees will sell at public auction on June 26, 2026, at 3:45 PM in front of the building housing the City of Richmond Circuit Court, 400 North 9th Street, at the entrance facing Clay Street (8th and Clay Streets), Richmond, VA 23219, the property designated in a deed recorded in: Book: 199 page: 1758, Lot: 12, Subdivision: Brookbury. Sale is subject to all prior liens, easements, restrictions, covenants, and conditions, if any, of record, or other matters which would be disclosed by an accurate survey or inspection of the premises. TERMS: CASH. A deposit of $6,000.00 or 10% of the sale price, whichever is lower, will be required of the successful bidder at time of sale. Prior to the sale, interested bidders will be required to register with and must present a bid deposit which may be held during the sale by the trustee. The bid deposit must be certified funds and/or cash, but no more than $9,900.00 of cash will be accepted. The successful bidder’s deposit will be retained at the sale and applied to the sale price. If held by the trustee, all other bid deposits will be returned to the unsuccessful bidders. Settlement is to be made within 15 calendar days. The successful bidder will be responsible for obtaining possession of the property, and for all costs and fees related to recording the Trustee’s Deed, including the grantors tax. The successful bidder will be required to execute a Memorandum of Trustee's Sale, available for review on the Foreclosure Sales page of www.glasserlaw.com, outlining additional terms of sale and settlement. A Trustee’s Deed will be prepared by Trustee’s attorney at high bidder’s expense. This is a communication from a debt collector, Glasser and Glasser, P.L.C. on behalf of Atlantic Trustee Services, L.L.C., and/or William K. Grogan, Attorney at Law, and/or NFPDS-VA LLC, Substitute Trustees, Crown Center Building, Suite 600, 580 East Main Street, Norfolk, VA 23510, File No. 241835-01, Tel: (757) 321-6465, between 10:00 a.m. & 12:00 noon only. COL-7006617 2026-05-15

listings.notice_text: Foreclosure Sale TRUSTEE’S SALE OF 5926 E STONEPATH GARDEN DRIVE, CHESTER, VA 23831 In execution of a certain Deed of Trust dated September 24, 2021, in the original principal amount of $36,837.00 recorded in the Clerk’s Office, Circuit Court for Chesterfield County, Virginia, in Book 13652 at Page 0071 as Instrument No. 210062552. The undersigned Substitute Trustee will offer for sale at public auction in the front of the Circuit Court building for the City of Richmond, John Marshall Courts Building, 400 North 9th Street, Richmond, Virginia, on June 8, 2026, at 2:30 PM, the property described in said Deed of Trust, located at the above address, and more particularly described as follows: CONDOMINIUM UNIT NO. 5926, PHASE 3, STONEBRIDGE GARDENS, A CONDOMINIUM COMMUNITY, TOGETHER WITH THE COMMON ELEMENT INTEREST AND ANY LIMITED COMMON ELEMENT INTEREST APPURTENANT THERETO, AS ESTABLISHED BY CONDOMINIUM INSTRUMENTS RECORDED IN DEED BOOK 8094, PAGE 646, AND ANY AND ALL SUPPLEMENTAL DECLARATIONS AND/OR AMENDMENTS RECORDED SUBSEQUENT THERETO IN THE CLERK`S OFFICE, CIRCUIT COURT, CHESTERFIELD COUNTY, VIRGINIA. TERMS OF SALE: ALL CASH. A bidder’s deposit of ten percent (10%) of the sale price or ten percent (10%) of the original principal balance of the subject Deed of Trust, whichever is lower, in the form of cash or certified funds payable to the Substitute Trustee must be present at the time of the sale. The balance of the purchase price will be due within fifteen (15) days of sale, otherwise Purchaser’s deposit may be forfeited to Trustee. Time is of the essence. Substitute Trustee has identified an unreleased security instrument which may be superior to the subject Deed of Trust. Substitute Trustee disclaims any implication that the Property will be sold free and clear of all liens. If the sale is set aside for any reason, the Purchaser at the sale shall be entitled to a return of the deposit paid. The Purchaser may, if provided by the terms of the Trustee’s Memorandum of Foreclosure Sale, be entitled to a $50 cancellation fee from the Substitute Trustee, but shall have no further recourse against the Mortgagor, the Mortgagee or the Mortgagee’s attorney. A form copy of the Trustee’s memorandum of foreclosure sale and contract to purchase real property is available for viewing at www.aldridgepite.com. Additional terms, if any, to be announced at the sale and the Purchaser may be given the option to execute the contract of sale electronically. This is a communication from a debt collector and any information obtained will be used for that purpose. The sale is subject to seller confirmation. Substitute Trustee: Equity Trustees, LLC, 8100 Three Chopt Road, Suite 240, Richmond, VA 23229. If you have any questions or concerns, please contact the Virginia Pre-Sale Department of counsel for Equity Trustees, LLC at 301-961-6555, website: www.aldridgepite.com. VA-373334-1.
